name: avb
detail: Enable Audio Video Bridging (AVB).
syntax: avb [ msrp-join-timer milliseconds | msrp-leave-timer milliseconds | msrp-leaveall-timer milliseconds | msrp-tx-slow | strict | vlan vlan-id ] ]
noForm: no avb [ msrp-join-timer | msrp-leave-timer | msrp-leaveall-timer | msrp-tx-slow | strict | vlan

params.name: msrp-join-timer milliseconds
params.description: (Optional) Configures the Multiple Stream Reservation Protocol (MSRP) join timer value in milliseconds. The range is from 100 to 4000. The default is 200.

params.name: msrp-leave-timer milliseconds
params.description: (Optional) Configures the MSRP leave timer value in milliseconds. The range is from 500 to 60000. The default is 1000.

params.name: msrp-leaveall-timer
params.description: (Optional) Configures the MSRP leaveall timer value in milliseconds. The

params.name: milliseconds
params.description: range is from 10000 to 50000. The default is 10000.

params.name: msrp-tx-slow
params.description: (Optional) Slows down the default packet sending rate with a gap of 100 milliseconds.

params.name: strict
params.description: (Optional) Configures the strict mode of AVB.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Configures a specified VLAN as the default AVB VLAN. The range is from from 2 to 4094.

name: channel-group
detail: Assign an Ethernet port to an EtherChannel group, or to enable an EtherChannel mode, or both.
syntax: channel-group channel-group-number mode {active | auto [non-silent] | desirable [non-silent] | on | passive}

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: Channel group number. The range is 1 to 128.

params.name: mode
params.description: Specifies the EtherChannel mode.

params.name: active
params.description: Unconditionally enables Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

params.name: auto
params.description: Enables the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) only if a PAgP device is detected.

params.name: non-silent
params.description: (Optional) Configures the interface for nonsilent operation when connected to a partner that is PAgP-capable. Use in PAgP mode with the auto or desirable keyword when traffic is expected from the other device.

params.name: desirable
params.description: Unconditionally enables PAgP.

params.name: on
params.description: Enables the on mode.

params.name: passive
params.description: Enables LACP only if a LACP device is detected.

usageSummary: For Layer 2 EtherChannels, the channel-group command automatically creates the port-channel interface when the channel group gets its first physical port.  You do not have to use the interface port-channel command Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: channel-protocol
detail: Restrict the protocol used on a port to manage channeling.

params.name: lacp
params.description: Configures an EtherChannel with the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP). pagp Configures an EtherChannel with the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP).

usageSummary: Use the channel-protocol command only to restrict a channel to LACP or PAgP.  If you set the protocol by using the channel-protocol command, the setting is not overridden by the channel-group command in interface configuration mode.  You must use the channel-group command in interface configuration mode to configure the EtherChannel parameters.

name: clear l2protocol-tunnel counters
detail: Clear the protocol counters in protocol tunnel ports.

params.name: interface-id
params.description: (Optional) The interface (physical interface or port cha counters are to be cleared.

usageSummary: Use this command to clear protocol tunnel counters on the switch or on the specified interface.  This example shows how to clear Layer 2 protocol tunnel counters on an interface: Device# clear l2protocol-tunnel counters gigabitethernet1/0/3 Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: clear lacp
detail: Clear Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) channel-group counters.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 128.

params.name: counters
params.description: Clears traffic counters.

usageSummary: You can clear all counters by using the clear lacp counters command, or you can clear only the counters for the specified channel group by using the clear lacp channel-group-number counters command.  This example shows how to clear all channel-group information: Device> enable Device# clear lacp counters This example shows how to clear LACP traffic counters for group 4: Device> enable Device# clear lacp 4 counters You can verify that the information was deleted by entering the show lacp counters or the show lacp channel-group-number counters command in privileged EXEC mode.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.

name: clear pagp
detail: Clear the Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) channel-group information.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: (Optional) Channel group number. The range is 1 to 128.

params.name: counters
params.description: Clears traffic counters.

usageSummary: You can clear all counters by using the clear pagp counters command, or you can clear only the counters for the specified channel group by using the clear pagp channel-group-number counters command.  This example shows how to clear all channel-group information: Device> enable Device# clear pagp counters This example shows how to clear PAgP traffic counters for group 10: Device> enable Device# clear pagp 10 counters You can verify that the information was deleted by entering the show pagp command in privileged EXEC mode.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.

name: clear spanning-tree counters
detail: Clear the spanning-tree counters.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: (Optional) Clears all spanning-tree counters on the specified include physical ports, VLANs, and port channels. The VLAN range is 1 to 4094. The port channel range is 1 to 128.

usageSummary: If the interface-id value is not specified, spanning-tree counters are cleared for all interfaces.  This example shows how to clear spanning-tree counters for all interfaces: Device> enable Device# clear spanning-tree counters Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: clear spanning-tree detected-protocols
detail: Restart the protocol migration process and force renegotiation with neighboring devices on the interface.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: (Optional) Restarts the protocol migration process on the specified in channels. The VLAN range is 1 to 4094. The port channel range is 1 to 128.

usageSummary: A device running the rapid per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (rapid-PVST+) protocol or the Multiple Spanning Tree Protocol (MSTP) supports a built-in protocol migration method that enables it to interoperate with legacy IEEE 802. 1D devices.  If a rapid-PVST+ or an MSTP device receives a legacy IEEE 802.

name: debug etherchannel
detail: Enable debugging of EtherChannels.
syntax: debug etherchannel [all | detail | error | event | idb ]
noForm: no debug etherchannel [all | detail | error | event | idb ]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all EtherChannel debug messages.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed EtherChannel debug messages.

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Displays EtherChannel error debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays EtherChannel event messages.

params.name: idb
params.description: (Optional) Displays PAgP interface descriptor block debug messages.

usageSummary: The undebug etherchannel command is the same as the no debug etherchannel command.

name: debug lacp
detail: Enable debugging of Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) activity.
syntax: debug lacp [all | event | fsm | misc | packet]
noForm: no debug lacp [all | event | fsm | misc | packet]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all LACP debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays LACP event debug messages.

params.name: fsm
params.description: (Optional) Displays messages about changes within the LACP finite state machine.

params.name: misc
params.description: (Optional) Displays miscellaneous LACP debug messages. packet (Optional) Displays the receiving and transmitting LACP control packets.

usageSummary: The undebug etherchannel command is the same as the no debug etherchannel command.  This example shows how to display all LACP debug messages: Device> enable Device# debug LACP all This example shows how to display debug messages related to LACP events: Device> enable Device# debug LACP event Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: debug pagp
detail: Enable debugging of Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) activity.
syntax: debug pagp [all | dual-active | event | fsm | misc | packet]
noForm: no debug pagp [all | dual-active | event | fsm | misc | packet]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all PAgP debug messages.

params.name: dual-active
params.description: (Optional) Displays dual-active detection messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays PAgP event debug messages.

params.name: fsm
params.description: (Optional) Displays messages about changes within the PAgP finite state machine.

params.name: misc
params.description: (Optional) Displays miscellaneous PAgP debug messages.

params.name: packet
params.description: (Optional) Displays the receiving and transmitting PAgP control packets.

usageSummary: The undebug pagp command is the same as the no debug pagp command.  This example shows how to display all PAgP debug messages: Device> enable Device# debug pagp all This example shows how to display debug messages related to PAgP events: Device> enable Device# debug pagp event Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: debug platform pm
detail: Enable debugging of the platform-dependent port manager software module.
syntax: debug platform pm {all | counters | errdisable | fec | if-numbers | l2-control | link-status | platform | pm-vectors [detail] | ses | vlans} | platform | pm-vectors [detail] | ses | vlans}
noForm: no debug platform pm {all | counters | errdisable | fec | if-numbers | l2-control | link-status

params.name: all
params.description: Displays all port manager debug messages.

params.name: counters
params.description: Displays counters for remote procedure call (RPC) debug messages.

params.name: errdisable
params.description: Displays error-disabled-related events debug messages.

params.name: fec
params.description: Displays forwarding equivalence class (FEC) platform-related events debug messages.

params.name: if-numbers
params.description: Displays interface-number translation event debug messages.

params.name: l2-control
params.description: Displays Layer 2 control infra debug messages.

params.name: link-status
params.description: Displays interface link-detection event debug messages.

params.name: platform
params.description: Displays port manager function event debug messages.

params.name: pm-vectors
params.description: Displays port manager vector-related event debug messages.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays vector-function details.

params.name: ses
params.description: Displays service expansion shelf (SES) related event debug messages.

params.name: vlans
params.description: Displays VLAN creation and deletion event debug messages.

usageSummary: The undebug platform pm command is the same as the no debug platform pm command.  This example shows how to display debug messages related to the creation and deletion of VLANs: Device> enable Device# debug platform pm vlans Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: debug platform udld
detail: Enable debugging of the platform-dependent UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) software.
noForm: no debug platform udld [error | event] [switch switch-number]

params.name: error
params.description: (Optional) Displays error condition debug messages.

params.name: event
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD-related platform event debug messages.

params.name: switch
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD debug messages for the specified stack member. switch-number

usageSummary: The undebug platform udld command is the same as the no debug platform udld command.  When you enable debugging on a switch stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.  To enable debugging on a stack member, you can start a session from the active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: debug spanning-tree
detail: Enable debugging of spanning-tree activities.
syntax: debug spanning-tree {all | backbonefast | bpdu | bpdu-opt | config | etherchannel | events | exceptions | general | ha | mstp | pvst+ | root | snmp | synchronization | switch | uplinkfast} exceptions | general | mstp | pvst+ | root | snmp | synchronization | switch | uplinkfast}
noForm: no debug spanning-tree {all | backbonefast | bpdu | bpdu-opt | config | etherchannel | events |

params.name: all
params.description: Displays all spanning-tree debug messages.

params.name: backbonefast
params.description: Displays BackboneFast-event debug messages.

params.name: bpdu
params.description: Displays spanning-tree bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) debug messages.

params.name: bpdu-opt
params.description: Displays optimized BPDU handling debug messages.

params.name: config
params.description: Displays spanning-tree configuration change debug messages.

params.name: etherchannel
params.description: Displays EtherChannel-support debug messages.

params.name: events
params.description: Displays spanning-tree topology event debug messages.

params.name: exceptions
params.description: Displays spanning-tree exception debug messages.

params.name: general
params.description: Displays general spanning-tree activity debug messages.

params.name: ha
params.description: Displays high-availability spanning-tree debug messages.

params.name: mstp
params.description: Debugs Multiple Spanning Tree Protocol (MSTP) events.

params.name: pvst+
params.description: Displays per-VLAN spanning-tree plus (PVST+) event debug messages.

params.name: root
params.description: Displays spanning-tree root-event debug messages.

params.name: snmp
params.description: Displays spanning-tree Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) handling debug messages.

params.name: switch
params.description: Displays switch shim command debug messages. This shim is the software module that is the interface between the generic Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) code and the platform-specific code of various device platforms.

params.name: synchronization
params.description: Displays the spanning-tree synchronization event debug messages.

params.name: uplinkfast
params.description: Displays UplinkFast-event debug messages. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 938 Layer 2/3 debug spanning-tree

usageSummary: The undebug spanning-tree command is the same as the no debug spanning-tree command.  When you enable debugging on a stack, it is enabled only on the active switch.  To enable debugging on the standby switch, start a session from the active switch by using the session switch-number command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: instance
detail: Map a VLAN or a group of VLANs to a multiple spanning tree (MST) instance.

params.name: instance-id
params.description: Instance to which the specified VLANs are mapped. The range is from 0 to 4094. vlans vlan-range Specifies the number of the VLANs to be mapped to the specified instance. The range is from 1 to 4094.

usageSummary: The vlans vlan-range is entered as a single value or a range.  The mapping is incremental, not absolute.  When you enter a range of VLANs, this range is added or removed to the existing instances.

name: interface port-channel
detail: Access or create a port channel.

params.name: port-channel-number
params.description: Channel group number. The range is 1 to 128.

usageSummary: For Layer 2 EtherChannels, you do not have to create a port-channel interface before assigning physical ports to a channel group.  Instead, you can use the channel-group command in interface configuration mode, which automatically creates the port-channel interface when the channel group obtains its first physical port.  If you create the port-channel interface first, the channel-group-number can be the same as the port-channel-number, or you can use a new number.

name: l2protocol-tunnel
detail: Enable tunneling of Layer 2 protocols on an access port, IEEE 802.1Q tunnel port, or a port channel.
syntax: l2protocol-tunnel [drop-threshold | shutdown-threshold] [value] [cdp | stp | vtp ] [lldp] [point-to-point | [pagp | lacp | udld]] [point-to-point | [pagp | lacp | udld]]

params.name: drop-threshold
params.description: (Optional) Sets a drop threshold for the maximum rate of Layer 2 protocol packets per second to be received before an interface drops packets. shutdown-threshold (Optional) Sets a shutdown threshold for the maximum rate of Layer 2 protocol packets per second to be received before an interface is shut down.

params.name: value
params.description: A threshold in packets per second to be received for encapsulation before the interface shuts down, or the threshold before the interface drops packets. The range is 1 to 4096. The default is no threshold.

params.name: cdp
params.description: (Optional) Enables tunneling of CDP, specifies a shutdown threshold for CDP, or specifies a drop threshold for CDP.

params.name: stp
params.description: (Optional) Enables tunneling of STP, specifies a shutdown threshold for STP, or specifies a drop threshold for STP.

params.name: vtp
params.description: (Optional) Enables tunneling or VTP, specifies a shutdown threshold for VTP, or specifies a drop threshold for VTP.

params.name: lldp
params.description: (Optional) Enables tunneling of LLDP packets.

params.name: point-to-point
params.description: (Optional) Enables point-to point tunneling of PAgP, LACP, and UDLD packets.

params.name: pagp
params.description: (Optional) Enables point-to-point tunneling of PAgP, specifies a shutdown threshold for PAgP, or specifies a drop threshold for PAgP.

params.name: lacp
params.description: (Optional) Enables point-to-point tunneling of LACP, specifies a shutdown threshold for LACP, or specifis a drop threshold for LACP.

params.name: udld
params.description: (Optional) Enables point-to-point tunneling of UDLD, specifies a shutdown threshold for UDLD, or specifies a drop threshold for UDLD.

modes: Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 944 Layer 2/3 l2protocol-tunnel
usageSummary: You can enable tunneling for Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP), Spanning Tree Protocol (STP), or VLAN Trunking Protocol (VTP) packets.  You can also enable point-to-point tunneling for Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP), Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP), or UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) packets.  You must enter this command, with or without protocol types, to tunnel Layer 2 packets.

name: lacp fast-switchover
detail: Enable Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) 1:1 link redundancy.

params.name: dampening
params.description: time Enables LACP 1:1 hot-standby dampening. The range is 30 to 180 seconds.

usageSummary: Prior to entering the lacp fast-switchover command, you must ensure the following: • The port channel protocol type is LACP.  • The lacp max-bundle 1 command has been entered on the port channel.

name: lacp max-bundle
detail: Define the maximum number of active LACP ports allowed in a port channel.

params.name: max_bundle_number
params.description: The maximum number of active LACP ports in the port channel. The range is 1 to 8. The default is 8.

usageSummary: An LACP channel group can have up to 16 Ethernet ports of the same type.  Up to eight ports can be active, and up to eight ports can be in hot-standby mode.  When there are more than eight ports in an LACP channel group, the device on the controlling end of the link uses port priorities to determine which ports are bundled into the channel and which ports are put in hot-standby mode.

name: lacp port-priority
detail: Configure the port priority for the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

params.name: priority
params.description: Port priority for LACP. The range is 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: The lacp port-priority command in interface configuration mode determines which ports are bundled and which ports are put in hot-standby mode when there are more than eight ports in an LACP channel group.  An LACP channel group can have up to 16 Ethernet ports of the same type.  Up to eight ports can be active, and up to eight ports can be in standby mode.

name: lacp rate
detail: Set the rate at which Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) control packets are ingressed to an LACP-supported interface.

params.name: fast
params.description: Specifies that LACP control packets are ingressed at the fast rate, once every 1 second.

usageSummary: Use this command to modify the duration of LACP timeout.  The LACP timeout value on Cisco switch is three times the LACP rate that is configured on the interface.  Using the lacp ratecommand, you can select the LACP timeout value for a switch to be either 90 seconds or 3 seconds.

name: lacp system-priority
detail: Configure the system priority for the Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP).

params.name: priority
params.description: System priority for LACP. The range is 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: The lacp system-priority command determines which device in an LACP link controls port priorities.  An LACP channel group can have up to 16 Ethernet ports of the same type.  Up to eight ports can be active, and up to eight ports can be in standby mode.

name: loopdetect
detail: Detect network loops.
noForm: no loopdetect [ time | action syslog | source-port ]

params.name: time
params.description: (Optional) Time interval at which loop-detect frames are sent, in seconds. Range: 0 to 10. Default: 5.

params.name: action syslog
params.description: (Optional) Displays a system message when a loop is detected.

params.name: source-port
params.description: (Optional) Error-disables the source port.

usageSummary: You can error-disable either the source port or the destination port depending on your requirement.  When the loopdetect command is configured without any of the keywords or variables, the feature is enabled and the destination port is error-disabled when a loop is detected. We recommend that you error-disable the source port to better control traffic flow to and from your network.

name: mvrp vlan creation
detail: Enable dynamic VLAN creation on a device using Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP).
usageSummary: MVRP dynamic VLAN creation can be used only if Virtual Trunking Protocol (VTP) is in transparent mode.

name: mvrp registration
detail: Set the registrars in a Multiple Registration Protocol (MRP) Attribute Declaration (MAD) instance associated with an interface.

params.name: normal
params.description: Registrar responds normally to incoming Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP) messages. Normal is the default state.

params.name: fixed
params.description: Registrar ignores all incoming MVRP messages and remains in the IN state.

params.name: forbidden
params.description: Registrar ignores all incoming MVRP messages and remains in the EMPTY (MT) state.

usageSummary: The mvrpregistration command is operational only if MVRP is configured on an interface.  The nomvrpregistration command sets the registrar state to the default (normal).  This command can be used to set the registrar in a MAD instance associated with an interface to one of the three states.

name: mvrp timer
detail: Set period timers that are used in Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol (MVRP) on a given interface.

params.name: join
params.description: Specifies the time interval between two transmit opportunities that are applied to the Applicant State Machine (ASMs).

params.name: leave
params.description: Specifies the duration time before a registrar is moved to EMPTY (MT) state from leave-all (LV) state.

params.name: leave-all
params.description: Specifies the time it takes for a LeaveAll timer to expire.

params.name: periodic
params.description: Sets the timer value to periodic, a fixed value of 100 centiseconds.

params.name: centiseconds
params.description: Timer value measured in centiseconds. • Join timer value range is 20 to 10000000. • Leave timer value range is 60 to 10000000. • LeaveAll timer value range is 10000 and 10000000. • Periodic timer value is fixed at 100 centiseconds.

usageSummary: The nomvrptimer command resets the timer value to the default value.

name: name
detail: Set the name of a Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) region.

params.name: name
params.description: Name to give the MST region. It can be any string with a maximum length of 32 characters.

usageSummary: Two or more devices with the same VLAN mapping and configuration version number are considered to be in different MST regions if the region names are different.

name: no ptp enable
detail: Disable PTP on an interface.

params.name: This
params.description: command has no aruguments or keywords.

name: pagp learn-method
detail: Learn the source address of incoming packets received from an EtherChannel port.

params.name: aggregation-port
params.description: Specifies address learning on the logical port channel. The device sends packets to the source using any port in the EtherChannel. This setting is the default. With aggregation-port learning, it is not important on which physical port the packet arrives.

params.name: physical-port
params.description: Specifies address learning on the physical port within the EtherChannel. The device sends packets to the source using the same port in the EtherChannel from which it learned the source address. The other end of the channel uses the same port in the channel for a particular destination MAC or IP address.

usageSummary: The learn method must be configured the same at both ends of the link.  The device supports address learning only on aggregate ports even though the physical-port keyword is provided in the command-line interface (CLI).  The pagp learn-method and the pagp port-priority commands in interface configuration mode have no effect on the device hardware, but they are required for PAgP interoperability with devices that only support address learning by physical ports.

name: pagp port-priority
detail: Select a port over which all Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) traffic through the EtherChannel is sent.

params.name: priority
params.description: Priority number. The range is from 0 to 255.

usageSummary: The physical port with the highest priority that is operational and has membership in the same EtherChannel is the one selected for PAgP transmission.  The device supports address learning only on aggregate ports even though the physical-port keyword is provided in the command-line interface (CLI).  The pagp learn-method and the pagp port-priority commands in interface configuration mode have no effect on the device hardware, but they are required for PAgP interoperability with devices that only support address learning by physical ports, such as the Catalyst 1900 switch.

name: peer
detail: Connect to a peer Precision Time Protocol-aware (PTP-aware) device.
syntax: peer { ip ip_address | vrf word ip ip_address }

params.name: ip ip_address
params.description: IP address of a peer PTP device.

params.name: vrf word
params.description: Default virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) or user-defined VRF.

usageSummary: You must configure the PTP-property name using the ptp property command and configure a unicast IPv4 connection from a loopback interface using the transport unicast ipv4 local loopback command before connecting to a peer PTP-aware device.

name: policy-map
detail: Enter policy-map configuration mode and create or modify a policy map that can be attached to one or more interfaces to specify a service policy.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the policy-map type.

params.name: access-control
params.description: (Optional) Enables the access-control specific policy map.

params.name: control subscriber
params.description: (Optional) Enables subscriber control policy domain.

params.name: packet-service
params.description: (Optional) Enables packet service policy map.

params.name: performance-monitor
params.description: (Optional) Enables policy map for the performance monitoring feature.

params.name: policy-map name
params.description: Specifies the policy map.

modes: Usage Guidelines Use the policy-map command to specify the name of the policy map to create (add or modify) before you configure policies for classes whose match criteria are defined in a class map with the class-map and match commands.
Note You can configure class policies in a policy map only if the classes have match criteria defined for them.
Note Because you can configure a maximum of 64 class maps, a policy map cannot contain more than 64 class policies.

A single policy map can be attached concurrently to more than one interface. Except as noted, when you attempt to attach a policy map to an interface, the attempt is denied if the available bandwidth on the interface cannot accommodate the total bandwidth requested by the multiple policies. In such cases, if the policy map is already attached to other interfaces, the map is removed.

usageSummary: Use the policy-map command to specify the name of the policy map to create (add or modify) before you configure policies for classes whose match criteria are defined in a class map with the class-map and match commands.

name: port-channel
detail: Convert the auto created EtherChannel into a manual channel and adding configuration on the EtherChannel.

params.name: channel-group-number
params.description: Channel group number. The range is 1 to 128.

params.name: persistent
params.description: Converts the auto created EtherChannel into a manual channel and allows you to add configuration on the EtherChannel.

usageSummary: You can use the show etherchannel summary command in privileged EXEC mode to display the EtherChannel information.

name: port-channel auto
detail: Enable the auto-LAG feature on a switch globally.
usageSummary: You can use the show etherchannel auto command in privileged EXEC mode to verify if the EtherChannel was created automatically.

name: port-channel load-balance
detail: Set the load-distribution method among the ports in the EtherChannel.
syntax: port-channel load-balance {dst-ip | dst-mac | dst-mixed-ip-port | dst-port | extended | src-dst-ip | src-dst-mac | src-dst-mixed-ip-port | src-dst-port | src-ip | src-mac | src-mixed-ip-port | src-port | vlan-dst-ip | vlan-dst-mixed-ip-port | vlan-src-dst-ip | vlan-src-dst-mixed-ip-port | vlan-src-ip | vlan-src-mixed-ip-port}

params.name: dst-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination host IP address.

params.name: dst-mac
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination host MAC address. Packets to the same destination are sent on the same port, but packets to different destinations are sent on different ports in the channel.

params.name: dst-mixed-ip-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination IPv4 or IPv6 address and the TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number.

params.name: dst-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number for both IPv4 and IPv6.

params.name: extended
params.description: Sets extended load balance methods among the ports in the EtherChannel.

params.name: src-dst-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source and destination host IP address.

params.name: src-dst-mac
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source and destination host MAC address.

params.name: src-dst-mixed-ip-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source and destination host IP address and TCP/UDP (layer 4) port number.

params.name: src-dst-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source and destination TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number.

params.name: src-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source host IP address.

params.name: src-mac
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source MAC address. Packets from different hosts use different ports in the channel, but packets from the same host use the same port.

params.name: src-mixed-ip-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source host IP address and TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number.

params.name: src-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number.

params.name: vlan-dst-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the VLAN ID and destination IP address. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 970 Layer 2/3 port-channel load-balance

params.name: vlan-dst-mixed-ip-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the VLAN ID, destination IP address, and TCP/UDP port number.

params.name: vlan-src-dst-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the VLAN ID, source and destination IP address. vlan-src-dst-mixed-ip-port Specifies load distribution based on the VLAN ID, source and destination IP address, and TCP/UDP port number.

params.name: vlan-src-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the VLAN ID and source IP address.

params.name: vlan-src-mixed-ip-port
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the VLAN ID, source IP address, and TCP/UDP port number.

usageSummary: You can verify your setting by entering either the show running-config command in privileged EXEC mode or the show etherchannel load-balance command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: port-channel load-balance extended
detail: Set combinations of load-distribution methods among the ports in the EtherChannel.
syntax: port-channel load-balance extended {dst-ip | dst-mac | dst-port | ipv6-label | l3-proto | src-ip | src-mac | src-port}

params.name: dst-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the destination host IP address. dst-mac Specifies load distribution based on the destination host MAC address. Packets to the same destination are sent on the same port, but packets to different destinations are sent on different ports in the channel. dst-port Specifies load distribution based on the destination TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number for both IPv4 and IPv6. ipv6-label Specifies load distribution based on the source MAC address and IPv6 flow label. This keyword is supported only on Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series Switches. l3-proto Specifies load distribution based on the source MAC address and Layer 3 protocols. This keyword is supported only on Cisco Catalyst 9500 Series Switches.

params.name: src-ip
params.description: Specifies load distribution based on the source host IP address. src-mac Specifies load distribution based on the source MAC address. Packets from different hosts use different ports in the channel, but packets from the same host use the same port. src-port Specifies load distribution based on the TCP/UDP (Layer 4) port number.

usageSummary: You can verify your setting by entering either the show running-config command in privileged EXEC mode or the show etherchannel load-balance command in privileged EXEC mode.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: port-channel min-links
detail: Define the minimum number of LACP ports that must be bundled in the link-up state and bundled in the EtherChannel in order that a port channel becomes active.

params.name: min_links_number
params.description: The minimum number of active LACP ports in the port channel. The range is 2 to 8 if the port channel number is 128 or lesser and the range is 2 to 4 if the port channel number is 129 or greater. The default is 1.

usageSummary: An LACP channel group can have up to 16 Ethernet ports of the same type.  Up to eight ports can be active, and up to eight ports can be in hot-standby mode.  When there are more than eight ports in an LACP channel group, the device on the controlling end of the link uses port priorities to determine which ports are bundled into the channel and which ports are put in hot-standby mode.

name: ptp dot1as extend property
detail: Extend IEEE 802.1AS profile to a Precision Time Protocol-property (PTP-property) name.

params.name: word
params.description: PTP property name

name: ptp ip dscp
detail: Configure IP DSCP value for PTP messages.
syntax: ptp ip dscp value message { event | general }
noForm: no ptp ip dscp value message { event | general }

params.name: value
params.description: IP DSCP value. The range is from 0 to 63. event Configures IP DSCP value for PTP event messages. general Configures IP DSCP value for PTP general messages.

usageSummary: Use this command for IEEE 1588 PTP profiles in IPv4 UDP transport mode only.

name: ptp priority1 value
detail: Specify the priority 1 value to use when advertising a PTP clock.

params.name: value
params.description: Specifies the priority 1 number to use for this clock. The range is 0 to 255. The default value is 128. Note If the value of priority1 is configured to 255, the clock cannot become as Grandmaster.

name: ptp priority2 value
detail: To specify the priority 2 number to use when advertising a PTP clock, use the ptp priority2 value command in global configuration mode

params.name: value
params.description: Specifies the priority 2 number to use for this clock. The range is 0 to 255. The default value is 128.

name: ptp profile dot1as
detail: Enable Generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) globally.

name: ptp property
detail: Set the Precision Time Protocol-property (PTP-property) name.

params.name: word
params.description: PTP-property name.

usageSummary: You can configure more than one IPv4 unicast connection that connects to a different boundary clock under the same property name.

name: ptp role primary
detail: Set an interface permanently as primary (master).
usageSummary: Setting a port permanently ensures that the port remain as a primary (master) even if a clock connected to the port can be elected as grandmaster clock.

name: rep admin vlan
detail: Configure a Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) administrative VLAN for the REP to transmit hardware flood layer (HFL) messages.
syntax: rep admin vlan vlan-id segment segment-id
noForm: no rep admin vlan vlan-id segment segment-id

params.name: vlan-id
params.description: 48-bit static MAC address.

params.name: segment
params.description: configures administrative VLAN for an REP segment.

params.name: segment-id
params.description: specifies the segment for which the admin VLAN has been assigned. Segment id number ranges from 1-1024

name: rep block port
detail: Configure Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) VLAN load balancing on a REP primary edge port.
syntax: rep block port {id port-id | neighbor-offset | preferred} vlan {vlan-list | all}
noForm: no rep block port {id port-id | neighbor-offset | preferred}

params.name: id port-id
params.description: Specifies the VLAN blocking alternate port by entering the unique port ID, which is automatically generated when REP is enabled. The REP port ID is a 16-character hexadecimal value. neighbor-offset VLAN blocking alternate port by entering the offset number of a neighbor. The range is from -256 to +256. A value of 0 is invalid.

params.name: preferred
params.description: Selects the regular segment port previously identified as the preferred alternate port for VLAN load balancing.

params.name: vlan
params.description: Identifies the VLANs to be blocked.

params.name: vlan-list
params.description: VLAN ID or range of VLAN IDs to be displayed. Enter a VLAN ID from 1 to 4094, or a range or sequence of VLANs (such as 1-3, 22, and 41-44) to be blocked.

params.name: all
params.description: Blocks all the VLANs.

usageSummary: When you select an alternate port by entering an offset number, this number identifies the downstream neighbor port of an edge port.  The primary edge port has an offset number of 1; positive numbers above 1 identify downstream neighbors of the primary edge port.  Negative numbers identify the secondary edge port (offset number -1) and its downstream neighbors.

name: rep lsl-age-timer
detail: Configure the Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) link status layer (LSL) age-out timer value.
usageSummary: While configuring REP configurable timers, we recommend that you configure the REP LSL number of retries first and then configure the REP LSL age-out timer value.

name: rep lsl-retries
detail: Configure the REP link status layer (LSL) number of retries.

params.name: number-of-retries
params.description: Number of LSL retries. The range of retries is from 3 to 10.

usageSummary: The rep lsl-retries command is used to configure the number of retries before the REP link is disabled.  While configuring REP configurable timers, we recommend that you configure the REP LSL number of retries first and then configure the REP LSL age-out timer value.  The following example shows how to configure REP LSL retries.

name: rep preempt delay
detail: Configure a waiting period after a segment port failure and recovery before Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) VLAN load balancing is triggered.
usageSummary: Enter this command on the REP primary edge port.  Enter this command and configure a preempt time delay for VLAN load balancing to be automatically triggered after a link failure and recovery.  If VLAN load balancing is configured after a segment port failure and recovery, the REP primary edge port starts a delay timer before VLAN load balancing occurs.

name: rep preempt segment
detail: Manually start Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) VLAN load balancing on a segment.

params.name: segment-id
params.description: ID of the REP segment. The range is from 1 to 1024.

usageSummary: Enter this command on the segment, which has the primary edge port on the device.  Ensure that all the other segment configuratios are completed before setting preemption for VLAN load balancing.  When you enter the rep preempt segment segment-id command, a confirmation message appears before the command is executed because preemption for VLAN load balancing can disrupt the network.

name: rep segment
detail: Enable Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) on an interface and to assign a segment ID to the interface.

params.name: segment-id
params.description: Segment for which REP is enabled. Assign a segment ID to the interface. The range is from 1 to 1024.

params.name: edge
params.description: (Optional) Configures the port as an edge port. Each segment has only two edge ports. no-neighbor (Optional) Specifies the segment edge as one with no external REP neighbor.

params.name: primary
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the port is the primary edge port where you can configure VLAN load balancing. A segment has only one primary edge port.

params.name: preferred
params.description: (Optional) Specifies that the port is the preferred alternate port or the preferred port for VLAN load balancing. Note Configuring a port as a preferred port does not guarantee that it becomes the alternate port; it merely gives it a slight edge among equal contenders. The alternate port is usually a previously failed port.

usageSummary: REP ports must be a Layer 2 IEEE 802. 1Q port or a 802. 1AD port.

name: rep stcn
detail: Configure a Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) edge port to send segment topology change notifications (STCNs) to another interface or to other segments.

params.name: interface interface-id
params.description: Specifies a physical interface or port channel to receive STCNs. segment segment-id-list Specifies one REP segment or a list of REP segments to receive STCNs. The segment range is from 1 to 1024. You can also configure a sequence of segments, for example, 3 to 5, 77, 100.

usageSummary: You can verify your settings by entering the show interfaces rep detail command in privileged EXEC mode.

name: revision
detail: Set the revision number for the Multiple Spanning Tree (802.1s) (MST) configuration.

params.name: version
params.description: Revision number for the configuration; valid values are from 0 to 65535.

usageSummary: Devices that have the same configuration but different revision numbers are considered to be part of two different regions.

name: show rep topology
detail: Display Resilient Ethernet Protocol (REP) topology information for a segment or for all the segments, including the primary and secondary edge ports in the segment.

params.name: segment segment-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the segment for which to display the REP topology information. The segment-id range is from 1 to 1024.

params.name: archive
params.description: (Optional) Displays the previous topology of the segment. This keyword is useful for troubleshooting a link failure.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed REP topology information.

name: show spanning-tree
detail: Display spanning-tree information for the specified spanning-tree instances.
syntax: show spanning-tree [bridge-group] [ active | backbonefast | blockedports | bridge [id] | detail | inconsistentports | instances | interface interface-type interface-number | mst [ list | configuration [digest] ] | pathcost method | root | summary [totals] | uplinkfast | vlan vlan-id ]

params.name: bridge-group
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the bridge group number. The range is 1 to 255.

params.name: active
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree information on active interfaces only.

params.name: backbonefast
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree BackboneFast status.

params.name: blockedports
params.description: (Optional) Displays blocked port information.

params.name: bridge
params.description: (Optional) Displays status and configuration of this switch.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Shows status and configuration details.

params.name: inconsistentports
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about inconsistent ports.

params.name: instances
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about maximum STP instances.

params.name: interface
params.description: interface-type            (Optional) Specifies the type and number of the interface. Enter each

params.name: interface-number
params.description: interface designator, using a space to separate it from the one before and the one after. Ranges are not supported. Valid interfaces include physical ports and virtual LANs (VLANs). See the “Usage Guidelines” for valid values.

params.name: mst
params.description: (Optional) Specifies multiple spanning-tree.

params.name: list
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a multiple spanning-tree instance list.

params.name: configuration digest
params.description: (Optional) Displays the multiple spanning-tree current region configuration.

params.name: pathcost method
params.description: (Optional) Displays the default path-cost calculation method that is used. See the “Usage Guidelines” section for the valid values.

params.name: root
params.description: (Optional) Displays root-switch status and configuration.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a summary of port states.

params.name: totals
params.description: (Optional) Displays the total lines of the spanning-tree state section.

params.name: uplinkfast
params.description: (Optional) Displays spanning-tree UplinkFast status.

params.name: vlan vlan-id
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the VLAN ID. The range is 1 to 4094. If the vlan-id value is omitted, the command applies to the spanning-tree instance for all VLANs.

params.name: id
params.description: (Optional) Identifies the spanning tree bridge. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1030 Layer 2/3 show spanning-tree

params.name: port-channel number
params.description: (Optional) Identifies the Ethernet channel associated with the interfaces.

usageSummary: The keywords and arguments that are available with the show spanning-tree command vary depending on the platform you are using and the network modules that are installed and operational.  The port-channel number values from 257 to 282 are supported on the Content Switching Module (CSM) and the Firewal Services Module (FWSM) only.  The interface-number argument designates the module and port number.

name: show spanning-tree mst
detail: Display the information about the Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) protocol.
syntax: show spanning-tree mst [ configuration [digest] | instance-id-number ] [ interface interface ] [ detail ] [ service instance ]

params.name: instance-id-number
params.description: (Optional) Instance identification number. The range is from 0 to 4094.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about the MST protocol.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays the information about the interfaces. See the “Usage Guidelines” section for valid number values.

params.name: configuration
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the region configuration.

params.name: digest
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the message digest 5 (MD5) algorithm included in the current MST configuration identifier (MSTCI).

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the interface type.

usageSummary: The valid values for the interface argument depend on the specified interface type and the chassis and module that are used.  For example, if you specify a Gigabit Ethernet interface and have a 48-port 10/100BASE-T Ethernet module that is installed in a 13-slot chassis, valid values for the module number are from 2 to 13 and valid values for the port number are from 1 to 48.  The number of valid values for port-channel number are a maximum of 64 values ranging from 1 to 282.

name: show udld
detail: Display UniDirectional Link Detection (UDLD) administrative and operational status for all ports or the specified port.
syntax: show udld [ANI | AccessTunnel | Auto-Template | BDI | CEM-PG | GMPLS | GigabitEthernet | HundredGigE | InternalInterface | LISP | Loopback | Null | PROTECTION_GROUP | Port-channel | SDH_ACR | SERIAL-ACR | Serial-PG | TLS-VIF | Tunnel | Tunnel-tp | TwentyFiveGigE | VirtualPortGroup | Vlan | nve] interface_number show udld neighbors show udld fast-hello interface_number

params.name: ANI
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Autonomic-Networking virtual interface.

params.name: AccessTunnel
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Access Tunnel Interface.

params.name: Auto-Template
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the auto-template interface. The range is from 1 to 999.

params.name: BDI
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Bridge-Domain interface.

params.name: CEM-PG
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Circuit Emulation interface with Protection group.

params.name: GMPLS
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the MPLS interface.

params.name: GigabitEthernet
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the GigabitEthernet interface.

params.name: HundredGigE
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Hundred Gigabit Ethernet.

params.name: InternalInterface
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the internal interface. The range is from 0 to 9.

params.name: LISP
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Locator/ID Separation Protocol Virtual Interface.

params.name: Loopback
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the loopback interface. The range is from 0 to 2147483647.

params.name: Null
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the null interface.

params.name: PROTECTION_GROUP
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Protection-group controller. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1039 Layer 2/3 show udld

params.name: Port-channel
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Ethernet channel interfaces. The range is 1 to 128.

params.name: SDH_ACR
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Virtual SDH-ACR controller.

params.name: SERIAL-ACR
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Serial interface with ACR.

params.name: Serial-PG
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Serial interface with Protection Group.

params.name: TLS-VIF
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the TLS Virtual Interface.

params.name: Tunnel
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the tunnel interface. The range is from 0 to 2147483647.

params.name: Tunnel-tp
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the MPLS Transport Profile interface.

params.name: TwentyFiveGigE
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Twenty Five Gigabit Ethernet.

params.name: VirtualPortGroup
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the Virtual Port Group.

params.name: Vlan
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of the VLAN interface. The range is from 1 to 4095.

params.name: interface_number
params.description: (Optional) ID of the interface and port number. Valid interfaces include physical ports, VLANs, and port channels.

params.name: nve
params.description: (Optional) Displays UDLD operational status of Network virtualization endpoint interface

params.name: neighbors
params.description: (Optional) Displays neighbor information only.

params.name: fast-hello
params.description: (Optional) Displays ports that have fast-hello configured and their fast-hello operational status.

params.name: fast-hello interface_number
params.description: (Optional) Displays the fast-hello information of a specific interface.

usageSummary: If you do not enter an interface ID, administrative and operational UDLD status for all interfaces appear.  Examples: This is an example of output from the show udld interface-id command.  For this display, UDLD is enabled on both ends of the link, and UDLD detects that the link is bidirectional.

name: show vlan dot1q tag native
detail: To display the status of tagging on the native VLAN use the show vlan dot1q tag native command.

name: source ip interface
detail: Configure the source IP address.

params.name: interface_id
params.description: Source IP address.

usageSummary: This command is optional.  Use this command as an alternative to the peer command.

name: spanning-tree backbonefast
detail: Enable BackboneFast to allow a blocked port on a switch to change immediately to a listening mode.
usageSummary: BackboneFast should be enabled on all of the Cisco devices containing an Ethernet switch network module.  BackboneFast provides for fast convergence in the network backbone after a spanning-tree topology change.  It enables the switch to detect an indirect link failure and to start the spanning-tree reconfiguration sooner than it would under normal spanning-tree rules.

name: spanning-tree bpdufilter
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) filtering on the interface.

params.name: enable
params.description: Enables BPDU filtering on this interface.

params.name: disable
params.description: Disables BPDU filtering on this interface.

usageSummary: Caution Be careful when you enter the spanning-tree bpdufilter enable command.  Enabling BPDU filtering on an interface is similar to disabling the spanning tree for this interface.  If you do not use this command correctly, you might create bridging loops.

name: spanning-tree bpduguard
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) guard on the interface.

params.name: enable
params.description: Enables BPDU guard on this interface.

params.name: disable
params.description: Disables BPDU guard on this interface.

usageSummary: BPDU guard prevents a port from receiving BPDUs.  Typically, this feature is used in a service-provider environment where the network administrator wants to prevent an access port from participating in the spanning tree.  If the port still receives a BPDU, it is put in the error-disabled state as a protective measure.

name: spanning-tree bpdu sender-conflict
detail: Enable spanning tree protocol BPDU sender conflict feature.
usageSummary: When the device is in RSTP mode, BPDU packets are transmitted every two seconds from a designated port to a non-designated port.  The arrival time of the messages can be monitored in the ports which are in the non-designated state.  When you use the spanning-tree bpdu sender-conflict command, if there is any change in the RSTP mode due to sender conflict, the device generates a notification.

name: spanning-tree bridge assurance
detail: Enable bridge assurance on all network ports on the device.
usageSummary: Bridge assurance protects against a unidirectional link failure or other software failure and a device that continues to forward data traffic when it is no longer running the spanning tree algorithm.  Bridge assurance is enabled only on spanning tree network ports that are point-to-point links.  Both ends of the link must have bridge assurance enabled.

name: spanning-tree cost
detail: Set the path cost of the interface for Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) calculations.

params.name: cost
params.description: Path cost. The range is from 1 to 200000000.

usageSummary: When you specify a value for the cost argument, higher values indicate higher costs.  This range applies regardless of the protocol type specified.  If a loop occurs, spanning tree uses the path cost when selecting an interface to place into the forwarding state.

name: spanning-tree etherchannel guard misconfig
detail: Display an error message when a loop due to a channel misconfiguration is detected.
usageSummary: EtherChannel uses either Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP) or Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) and does not work if the EtherChannel mode of the interface is enabled using the channel-group group-number mode on command.  The spanning-tree etherchannel guard misconfig command detects two types of errors: misconfiguration and misconnection errors.  A misconfiguration error is an error between the port-channel and an individual port.

name: spanning-tree extend system-id
detail: Enable the extended-system ID feature on chassis that support 1024 MAC addresses.
usageSummary: Enabling or disabling the extended-system ID updates the bridge IDs of all active Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) instances, which might change the spanning-tree topology.

name: spanning-tree guard
detail: Enable or disable the guard mode.

params.name: loop
params.description: Enables the loop-guard mode on the interface.

params.name: root
params.description: Enables root-guard mode on the interface.

params.name: none
params.description: Sets the guard mode to none.

name: spanning-tree link-type
detail: Configure a link type for a port.

params.name: point-to-point
params.description: Specifies that the interface is a point-to-point link.

params.name: shared
params.description: Specifies that the interface is a shared medium.

usageSummary: Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol Plus (RSTP+) fast transition works only on point-to-point links between two bridges.  By default, the switch derives the link type of a port from the duplex mode.  A full-duplex port is considered as a point-to-point link while a half-duplex configuration is assumed to be on a shared link.

name: spanning-tree loopguard default
detail: Enable loop guard as a default on all ports of a given bridge.
usageSummary: Loop guard provides additional security in the bridge network.  Loop guard prevents alternate or root ports from becoming the designated port due to a failure that could lead to a unidirectional link.  Loop guard operates only on ports that are considered point to point by the spanning tree.

name: spanning-tree mode
detail: Switch between Per-VLAN Spanning Tree+ (PVST+), Rapid-PVST+, and Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) modes.

params.name: pvst
params.description: (Optional) PVST+ mode.

params.name: mst
params.description: (Optional) MST mode.

params.name: rapid-pvst
params.description: (Optional) Rapid-PVST+ mode.

usageSummary: Note Be careful when using the spanning-tree mode command to switch between PVST+, Rapid-PVST+, and MST modes.  When you enter the command, all spanning-tree instances are stopped for the previous mode and are restarted in the new mode.  Using this command may cause disruption of user traffic.

name: spanning-tree mst
detail: Set the priority parameters or configure the device as a root for any Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) instance.
syntax: spanning-tree mst instance-id { priority priority | root { primary | secondary } }
noForm: no spanning-tree mst instance-id { { priority priority | root { primary | secondary } } }

params.name: priority priority
params.description: Port priority for an instance. The range is from 0 to 61440 in increments of 4096.

params.name: root
params.description: Configures the device as a root.

name: spanning-tree mst configuration
detail: Enter MST-configuration submode.
usageSummary: The MST configuration consists of three main parameters: • Instance VLAN mapping: See the instance command.  • Region name: See the name command (MST configuration submode).  • Configuration revision number: See the revision command.

name: spanning-tree mst forward-time
detail: Set the forward-delay timer for all the instances on the device.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Number of seconds to set the forward-delay timer for all the instances on the device. The range is from 4 to 30 seconds.

name: spanning-tree mst hello-time
detail: Set the hello-time delay timer for all the instances on the device.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Number of seconds to set the hello-time delay timer for all the instances on the device. The range is from 1 to 10 in seconds.

usageSummary: If you do not specify the hello-time value, the value is calculated from the network diameter.

name: spanning-tree mst max-age
detail: Set the max-age timer for all the instances on the device.

params.name: seconds
params.description: Number of seconds to set the max-age timer for all the instances on the device. The range is from 6 to 40 in seconds.

name: spanning-tree mst max-hops
detail: Specify the number of possible hops in the region before a bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) is discarded.

params.name: hopnumber
params.description: Number of possible hops in the region before a BPDU is discarded. The range is from 1 to 255 hops.

name: spanning-tree mst pre-standard
detail: Configure a port to transmit only prestandard bridge protocol data units (BPDUs).
usageSummary: Even with the default configuration, the port can receive both prestandard and standard BPDUs.  Prestandard BPDUs are based on the Cisco IOS Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) implementation that was created before the IEEE standard was finalized.  Standard BPDUs are based on the finalized IEEE standard.

name: spanning-tree mst priority
detail: Set the bridge priority for an instance.

params.name: instance
params.description: Instance identification number; valid values are from 0 to 4094. priority priority Specifies the bridge priority; see the “Usage Guidelines” section for valid values and additional information.

usageSummary: You can set the bridge priority in increments of 4096 only.  When you set the priority, valid values are 0, 4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480, 24576, 28672, 32768, 36864, 40960, 45056, 49152, 53248, 57344, and 61440.  You can set the priority to 0 to make the switch root.

name: spanning-tree mst root
detail: Designate the primary and secondary root switch and set the timer value for an instance.
syntax: spanning-tree mst instance root { primary | secondary } [ diameter diameter [ hello-time seconds ]]

params.name: instance
params.description: Instance identification number. The range is from 0 to 4094.

params.name: primary
params.description: Specifies the high enough priority (low value) to make the root of the spanning-tree instance.

params.name: secondary
params.description: Specifies the switch as a secondary root, should the primary root fail.

params.name: diameter diameter
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the timer values for the root switch that are based on the network diameter. The range is from 1 to 7.

params.name: hello-time seconds
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the duration between the generation of configuration messages by the root switch.

usageSummary: You can enter instance as a single instance or a range of instances, for example, 0-3,5,7-9.  The spanning-tree mst root secondary value is 16384.  The diameter diameter and hello-time seconds keywords and arguments are available for instance 0 only.

name: spanning-tree mst simulate pvst global
detail: To enable Per-VLAN Spanning Tree (PVST) simulation globally, enter the spanning-tree mst simulate pvst global command in global configuration mode. To disable PVST simulation globally, enter the no form of this command.
noForm: no spanning-tree mst simulate pvst global
usageSummary: PVST simulation is enabled by default so that all interfaces on the device interoperate between Multiple Spanning Tree (MST) and Rapid Per-VLAN Spanning Tree Plus (PVST+).  To prevent an accidental connection to a device that does not run MST as the default Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) mode, you can disable PVST simulation.  If you disable PVST simulation, the MST-enabled port moves to the blocking state once it detects it is connected to a Rapid PVST+-enabled port.

name: spanning-tree pathcost method
detail: Set the default path-cost calculation method.

params.name: long
params.description: Specifies the 32-bit based values for default port-path costs.

params.name: short
params.description: Specifies the 16-bit based values for default port-path costs.

usageSummary: The long path-cost calculation method utilizes all 32 bits for path-cost calculation and yields values in the range of 1 through 200,000,000.  The short path-cost calculation method (16 bits) yields values in the range of 1 through 65535.

name: spanning-tree port-priority
detail: Set an interface priority when two bridges tie for position as the root bridge.

params.name: port-priority
params.description: Port priority. The range is from 0 to 240 in increments of 16 . The default is 128.

usageSummary: The priority you set breaks the tie between two bridges to be designated as a root bridge.

name: spanning-tree portfast edge bpdufilter default
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) filtering by default on all PortFast ports.
noForm: no spanning-tree portfast edge bpdufilter default
usageSummary: The spanning-tree portfast edge bpdufilter command enables BPDU filtering globally on PortFast ports.  BPDU filtering prevents a port from sending or receiving any BPDUs.  You can override the effects of the portfast edge bpdufilter default command by configuring BPDU filtering at the interface level.

name: spanning-tree portfast edge bpduguard default
detail: Enable bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) guard by default on all PortFast ports.
noForm: no spanning-tree portfast edge bpduguard default
usageSummary: Caution Be careful when using this command.  You should use this command only with interfaces that connect to end stations; otherwise, an accidental topology loop could cause a data-packet loop and disrupt the device and network operation.  BPDU guard disables a port if it receives a BPDU.

name: spanning-tree portfast default
detail: Enable PortFast by default on all access ports.
syntax: spanning-tree portfast { edge [ bpdufilter | bpduguard ] | network | normal } default
noForm: no spanning-tree portfast { edge [ bpdufilter | bpduguard ] | network | normal } default

params.name: bpdufilter
params.description: Enables PortFast edge BPDU filter by default on all PortFast edge ports.

params.name: bpduguard
params.description: Enables PortFast edge BPDU guard by default on all PortFast edge ports.

params.name: edge
params.description: Enables PortFast edge mode by default on all switch access ports.

params.name: network
params.description: Enables PortFast network mode by default on all switch access ports.

params.name: normal
params.description: Enables PortFast normal mode by default on all switch access ports.

modes: Cisco IOS XE Everest 16.5.1a This command was introduced.

Note Be careful when using this command. You should use this command only with interfaces that connect to end stations; otherwise, an accidental topology loop could cause a data-packet loop and disrupt the operation of the router or switch and the network.

An interface with PortFast mode enabled is moved directly to the spanning-tree forwarding state when linkup occurs without waiting for the standard forward-time delay. You can enable PortFast mode on individual interfaces using the spanning-tree portfast (interface) command.
Examples This example shows how to enable PortFast edge mode with BPDU Guard by default on all access ports:
Device(config)# spanning-tree portfast edge bpduguard default Device(config)#
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1080 Layer 2/3 spanning-tree portfast default
show spanning-tree Displays information about the spanning-tree state.
spanning-tree portfast (interface) Enables PortFast on a specific interface.
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1081 Layer 2/3
usageSummary: Note Be careful when using this command.  You should use this command only with interfaces that connect to end stations; otherwise, an accidental topology loop could cause a data-packet loop and disrupt the operation of the router or switch and the network.  An interface with PortFast mode enabled is moved directly to the spanning-tree forwarding state when linkup occurs without waiting for the standard forward-time delay.

name: spanning-tree transmit hold-count
detail: Specify the transmit hold count.

params.name: value
params.description: Number of bridge protocol data units (BPDUs) that can be sent before pausing for 1 second. The range is from 1 to 20.

usageSummary: This command is supported on all spanning-tree modes.  The transmit hold count determines the number of BPDUs that can be sent before pausing for 1 second.

name: spanning-tree uplinkfast
detail: Enable UplinkFast.
usageSummary: Use the spanning-tree uplinkfast max-update-rate command to enable UplinkFast (if it is not already enabled) and change the rate at which update packets are sent.  Use the no form of this command to return to the default rate.

name: spanning-tree vlan
detail: Configure Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) on a per-virtual LAN (VLAN) basis.
syntax: spanning-tree vlan vlan-id [ forward-time seconds | hello-time seconds | max-age seconds | priority priority | root [ primary | secondary ] ]
noForm: no spanning-tree vlan vlan-id [ forward-time | hello-time | max-age | priority | root ]

params.name: vlan id
params.description: VLAN identification number. The range is from 1 to 4094. forward-time seconds (Optional) Sets the STP forward delay time. The range is from 4 to 30 seconds.

params.name: hello-time seconds
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the duration, in seconds, between the generation of configuration messages by the root switch. The range is from 1 to 10 seconds.

params.name: max-age seconds
params.description: (Optional) Sets the maximum number of seconds the information in a bridge packet data unit (BPDU) is valid. the range is from 6 to 40 seconds.

params.name: priority priority
params.description: (Optional) Sets the STP bridge priority. the range is from 0 to 65535.

params.name: root primary
params.description: (Optional) Forces this switch to be the root bridge.

params.name: root secondary
params.description: (Optional) Specifies this switch to act as the root switch should the primary root fail.

modes: Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1084 Layer 2/3 spanning-tree vlan
Cisco IOS XE Everest 16.5.1a This command was introduced.

Caution • When disabling spanning tree on a VLAN using the no spanning-tree vlan vlan-id command, ensure that all switches and bridges in the VLAN have spanning tree disabled. You cannot disable spanning tree on some switches and bridges in a VLAN and leave it enabled on other switches and bridges in the same VLAN because switches and bridges with spanning tree enabled have incomplete information about the physical topology of the network. • We do not recommend disabling spanning tree, even in a topology that is free of physical loops. Spanning tree is a safeguard against misconfigurations and cabling errors. Do not disable spanning tree in a VLAN without ensuring that there are no physical loops present in the VLAN.

When you set the max-age seconds parameter, if a bridge does not hear bridge protocol data units (BPDUs) from the root bridge within the specified interval, it assumes that the network has changed and recomputes the spanning-tree topology. The spanning-tree root primary command alters this switch’s bridge priority to 8192. If you enter the spanning-tree root primary command and the switch does not become the root switch, then the bridge priority is changed to 100 less than the bridge priority of the current bridge. If the switch still does not become the root, an error results. The spanning-tree root secondary command alters this switch’s bridge priority to 16384. If the root switch should fail, this switch becomes the next root switch. Use the spanning-tree root commands on backbone switches only. The spanning-tree etherchannel guard misconfig command detects two types of errors: misconfiguration and misconnection errors. A misconfiguration error is an error between the port-channel and an individual port. A misconnection error is an error between a switch that is channeling more ports and a switch that is not using enough Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) Bridge Protocol Data Units (BPDUs) to detect the error. In this case, the switch will only error disable an EtherChannel if the switch is a nonroot switch.
Examples The following example shows how to enable spanning tree on VLAN 200:
The following example shows how to configure the switch as the root switch for VLAN 10 with a network diameter of 4:
Device(config)# spanning-tree vlan 10 root primary diameter 4
The following example shows how to configure the switch as the secondary root switch for VLAN 10 with a network diameter of 4:
Device(config)# spanning-tree vlan 10 root secondary diameter 4
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1085 Layer 2/3 spanning-tree vlan

spanning-tree uplinkfast Enables the UplinkFast feature.
show spanning-tree Displays spanning-tree information for the specified spanning-tree instances.
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1086 Layer 2/3 switchport
